key_insight: A static 5D interpolation-table surrogate with periodic linear recalibration can approximate VPSC-level irradiation-driven viscoplastic behavior in zirconium alloys while avoiding the cost of full multiscale coupling.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster FEM analysis of nuclear core components such as spacer grids and fuel cladding, supporting design and safety assessments over reactor lifetimes.
method: Embed a Polycrystal Yield Surface (PCYS) interpolation table into Code_Aster as a material surrogate.
Update the surrogate through periodic recalibration plus first-order Taylor linearization to track irradiation-induced microstructural drift.
Benchmark accuracy and runtime against VPSC-FEM simulations under continuous high-dose irradiation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports relative errors below 1% for representative deformation paths and meaningful computational savings in spacer-grid structural simulations.
watch_item: The approach depends on periodic recalibration and may require careful validation to remain reliable when loading paths or irradiation conditions depart from the trained table.
paper_title: Accelerating Multi-scale Simulations of Nuclear Components via PCYS Interpolation Tables
